title: Adjusting: Supplies Used
event: Reyes Co. purchased $2,500 of supplies during the year (debited to Supplies). A year-end count shows $900 of supplies still on hand. Prepare the adjusting entry.

lines.label: 2,500 − 900
lines.trap: The count gives you what's LEFT — you must back into what was used. Students credit the 900 constantly.

lines.label: down to counted balance
lines.why: Brings the asset down to the $900 the count actually found.
lines.trap: Adjust BY the used amount so the balance lands AT the counted amount — two different numbers.

memorize.body: Supplies used = available − ending count. Adjust BY the used amount, TO the counted amount.
Same pattern as prepaid insurance — the count replaces the calendar as the measuring stick.

questions.prompt: Supplies available $2,500, count shows $900 on hand. Supplies expense = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the ending balance, not the expense. The expense is what disappeared: 2,500 − 900 = 1,600.
You expensed everything bought. $900 is still sitting on the shelf as an asset.

build.scaffold: Available minus counted = used. The entry amount is the USED figure.
